_meta.provenance: Public domain (U.S. federal government work, 17 U.S.C. 105)
_meta.note: Summaries are representative excerpts of public Medicare Administrative Contractor (MAC) LCD language, cached so Track 1 requires no live CMS API call.

lcds.title: Major Joint Replacement (Hip and Knee)
lcds.summary: Total knee arthroplasty (CPT 27447) is reasonable and necessary for advanced osteoarthritis (e.g., ICD-10 M17.x) when imaging confirms joint-space narrowing and the record documents failure of conservative management — including a trial of anti-inflammatory medication, activity modification, and physical therapy — over a sustained period. The conservative-care trial period is the most frequently cited documentation gap.

lcds.title: Major Joint Replacement — Southeast Region
lcds.summary: Covers CPT 27447 for documented end-stage degenerative joint disease. Requires radiographic evidence and documentation of failed non-operative therapy. Body Mass Index and smoking status are advisory documentation elements that strengthen the determination but do not block it.

lcds.title: Evaluation and Management Services
lcds.summary: Established-patient office visits (CPT 99213) require documentation supporting the level of service: a relevant history, examination, and medical decision-making of low complexity. No prior authorization required.
